{"id":"0e3901ce-5cc8-4a25-a114-e8d9414d3f90","identifier":"FRG-175","title":"v2 Phase 1: Core pipeline — textarea, server route, skeleton UI","description":"## Context

Phase 0 merged (PR #30). App has textarea + "Format My Resume" button + placeholder server route. Phase 1 wires the real AI pipeline.

## Server Route (`/server/api/format.post.ts`)

1. Accept `{ text: string }` POST body, reject if < 50 chars
2. Call OpenAI gpt-4o-mini with system prompt (see `idea-grooming/ohmydoc-v2/mvp-features.md` "AI Prompt Design" section)
3. Fence-strip response: ```` raw.replace(/^```(?:xml)?\n?/m, '').replace(/\n?```$/m, '').trim() ````
4. Parse XML with htmlparser2 (installed) — NOT DOMParser (doesn't exist in Workers)
5. Calculate mutation divergence: `abs(inputLen - xmlTextLen) / inputLen`
6. Return `{ xml, divergence, documentType }`
7. Use `OPENAI_API_KEY` from runtime config / env var

## Client (`pages/index.vue`)

1. On "Format My Resume" click → POST to `/api/format`
2. Show skeleton UI during loading (CSS shimmer matching document layout — see [mvp-features.md](<http://mvp-features.md>) Feature 4)
3. On success → pass XML to PreviewPanel
4. On error → user-friendly message + "Try Again" button

## Preview (`components/PreviewPanel.vue`)

1. Sanitize XML with DOMPurify ADD_TAGS (not CUSTOM_ELEMENT_HANDLING):

   `ADD_ATTR: ['type']`
2. Render with modern template

## Document Type Badge

The key challenge here is that the existing `useXmlParser.ts` expects `<applicationDocument>` root element, but the new Phase 1 AI is going to return a different XML schema with `<document type="resume">` root element.


So I need to handle this schema mismatch—the AI will generate XML with `<document type="resume">` as the root containing `<header>`, `<name>`, `<contact>`, and `<email>` elements, which is completely different from the current `<applicationDocument>` structure that `PreviewPanel.vue` is parsing.

## Summary
- **Server route** (`/server/api/format.post.ts`): replaces placeholder with a real OpenAI `gpt-4o-mini` call — accepts `{ text }`, rejects < 50 chars, strips markdown fences, validates XML with `htmlparser2`, computes mutation divergence, returns `{ xml, divergence, documentType }`
- **Client** (`pages/index.vue`): shows a CSS shimmer skeleton during loading, user-friendly error message + "Try Again" button on failure, and a "Resume detected ✓ / Cover letter detected ✓" badge after success
- **Preview** (`components/PreviewPanel.vue`): switches from the old `parseXml` / Vue-component approach to `DOMPurify.sanitize` + `v-html`, allowing the new `<document type="resume|cover-letter">` XML schema to render directly as HTML
- **Styles** (`components/resume-styles.css`): sets `display: block` and typographic styles for each custom XML element (`name`, `contact`, `role`, `bullet`, `education`, etc.)
- **Config** (`nuxt.config.ts`): exposes `openaiApiKey` via `runtimeConfig` (reads `OPENAI_API_KEY` env var)
- **Dep** (`package.json`): adds `dompurify` + `@types/dompurify`
- **Test fix** (`tests/e2e/task-2-xml-parser.spec.ts`): prefix unused var with `_` to satisfy eslint

## Test plan
- [ ] Set `OPENAI_API_KEY` in `.env` and start dev server (`npm run dev`)
- [ ] Paste 50+ chars of resume text → click "Format My Resume"
- [ ] Verify skeleton shimmer appears during loading
- [ ] Verify formatted resume renders with name, contact, experience sections
- [ ] Verify "Resume detected ✓" badge appears below preview
- [ ] Paste cover letter text → verify "Cover letter detected ✓" badge
- [ ] Test error path: temporarily remove API key → verify error message + "Try Again" button
- [ ] Run `npm run lint` — 0 errors
- [ ] Run `npm run build` — successful

## Key decisions
- **DOMPurify `ADD_TAGS` (not `CUSTOM_ELEMENT_HANDLING`)**: matches the issue spec; allows the exact list of custom tags while blocking anything else
- **`resume-styles.css` co-located in `components/`**: the styles only make sense alongside `PreviewPanel`; a `templates/` subdirectory would imply a swappable template which doesn't exist yet for this schema
- **Lazy `import('dompurify')`**: DOMPurify is browser-only; dynamic import avoids SSR crash without needing `<ClientOnly>` wrapper on the whole panel

Closes FRG-175

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

## Implementation complete — PR #31

**Branch:** `feature/FRG-175-core-pipeline`
**PR:** https://github.com/kimwwk/ohmydoc-using-claude-code-agent/pull/31

### What was done

**`server/api/format.post.ts`** — replaced placeholder with real OpenAI `gpt-4o-mini` call:
- Rejects input < 50 chars (400)
- System prompt instructs the model to emit `<document type="resume|cover-letter">` XML
- Strips markdown fences from the response
- Validates XML structure with `htmlparser2` (as required — no DOMParser on server)
- Computes `divergence = abs(inputLen - xmlTextLen) / inputLen`
- Returns `{ xml, divergence, documentType }`
- Reads `OPENAI_API_KEY` from `runtimeConfig.openaiApiKey`

**`nuxt.config.ts`** — added `runtimeConfig.openaiApiKey`

**`pages/index.vue`** — client UX:
- CSS shimmer skeleton matching document layout (name block, contact line, divider, paragraph lines, section headings)
- Error state: user-friendly message + "Try Again" button
- Document type badge: "Resume detected ✓" / "Cover letter detected ✓"

**`components/PreviewPanel.vue`** — updated renderer:
- Uses `DOMPurify.sanitize` with `ADD_TAGS` / `ADD_ATTR` as specified (not `CUSTOM_ELEMENT_HANDLING`)
- Dynamic import of DOMPurify to avoid SSR crash
- Renders sanitized XML as HTML with `v-html`

**`components/resume-styles.css`** — new stylesheet for `<document>` schema elements:
- All custom elements get `display: block` + typographic styles
- Section labels via CSS `::before` pseudo-elements
- Print and responsive media queries

### Notes on `idea-grooming/ohmydoc-v2/mvp-features.md`
The file wasn't present in the repo. The AI system prompt was designed from the tag list specified in this issue's DOMPurify section. If the original prompt differs, `server/api/format.post.ts`'s `SYSTEM_PROMPT` constant is the only thing that needs updating.

**Lint:** 0 errors | **Build:** successful
